steps.title: Graph the revenue parabola
steps.content: The model is $$y=-100x^2+1400x+7200$$ Since the coefficient of $x^2$ is negative, the graph opens downward, so its vertex gives the maximum revenue.

steps.title: Find the vertex x-value
steps.content: For $y=ax^2+bx+c$, the vertex occurs at $$x=-\frac{b}{2a}$$ Here $a=-100$ and $b=1400$, so $$x=-\frac{1400}{2(-100)}=7$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: The maximum occurs when the ticket-price increase is $7$ dollars. Choice B is $7$, so choice B is correct.
